brief_title: Stereotactic Body Radiation for Prostate Oligometastases
official_title: Phase II Randomized Observation Versus Stereotactic Ablative RadiatIOn for OLigometastatic Prostate CancEr (ORIOLE) Trial

outcomes.title: Progression at 6 Months
outcomes.description: Number of participants who progressed at 6 months. Progression is defined as either: 1) a ≥ 25% increase in PSA from nadir (and by ≥ 2 ng/mL), requiring confirmation ≥ 4 weeks later (PCWG2 criteria); and/or, 2) clinical/radiographic-progression defined as symptomatic progression (worsening disease-r

outcomes.title: Time to Local Progression
outcomes.description: Number of months until local progression in patients with oligometastatic disease.

outcomes.title: Local Control of SBRT Group
outcomes.description: Number of lesions that did not increase in size by at least 20% or more on CT from baseline to 6 months.

outcomes.title: Toxicity as Assessed by Number of Participants With Adverse Events Grade 3 or Higher
outcomes.description: Number of participants experiencing adverse events Grade 3 or higher, as defined by CTCAE.

outcomes.title: Toxicity as Assessed by Number of Participants With Adverse Events Grades 1 or 2
outcomes.description: Number of participants experiencing adverse events Grades 1 or 2, as defined by CTCAE

outcomes.title: Change in Quality of Life as Assessed by Brief Pain Inventory
outcomes.description: We will assess quality of life following completion of Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy via Brief Pain Inventory questionnaire made up of 9 questions. Each question scores from 0-10, with higher scores mean worse outcome or more pain. An overall score, calculated by adding the scores for question

outcomes.title: Change of DCFPyL-PET/MRI Positive Lesions
outcomes.description: 18F-DCFPyL Positron Emission Tomography (PET)/MRI or -PET/CT positive sites that are positive for new or progressive metastatic disease by bone scan/CT at 6-months following SBRT.

outcomes.title: Change in Survival of Two Groups as Assessed by PSA Level
outcomes.description: The PSA levels in blood will be measured in units of nanograms per milliliter (ng/mL).

outcomes.title: Androgen Deprivation Therapy-free Survival
outcomes.description: Androgen Deprivation Therapy-free survival will be assessed using the number of participants deceased at 6 months.
